ddls:
CREATE TABLE "revisions" (
  "revision_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each revision */,
  "software_version" INTEGER /* ID of the software version */,
  "revision_number" INTEGER /* Number of the revision */,
  "revision_date" TEXT /* Date the revision was made */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who made the revision */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the changes in the revision */,
  "change_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the type of change */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the revision (e.g., Draft, Reviewed, Published) */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reviewer */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the revision was reviewed */,
  "approval_status" TEXT /* Approval status of the revision (e.g., Approved, Rejected) */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date the revision was approved */,
  "change_summary" TEXT /* Brief summary of the changes */,
  "related_issue_ids" TEXT /* Related issue or bug tracking IDs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("revision_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_revisions_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_revisions_software_version FOREIGN KEY ("software_version") REFERENCES software_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_revisions_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_revisions_change_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_type_id") REFERENCES change_types ("change_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "software_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each software version */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the software */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., Active, Deprecated, In Development) */,
  "release_notes" TEXT /* Detailed release notes for the version */,
  "deprecation_date" TEXT /* Date the version was deprecated */,
  "documentation_link" TEXT /* Link to the official documentation for the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the author belongs to */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title or position of the author */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the author */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the author (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_types" (
  "change_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type of change */,
  "change_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the change type (e.g., Update, New Feature, Bug Fix) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the change type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "revision_id" INTEGER /* ID of the revision the resource is linked to */,
  "resource_path" TEXT /* Path to the resource file or link */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of resource (e.g., PDF, Video, Code Example) */,
  "resource_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the resource */,
  "resource_size" TEXT /* Size of the resource file */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the resource was uploaded */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the resource */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resources_revision_id FOREIGN KEY ("revision_id") REFERENCES revisions ("revision_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resources_uploaded_by FOREIGN KEY ("uploaded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, writer, end-user) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Permission level of the user (e.g., Admin, Editor, Viewer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
